legal.contactText: For data-related questions, contact the responsible department administrator or use the university's official contact channel.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: This page explains what data is used in the graduate employment tracking system and how access to it is protected.
privacy.summaryText: The system processes only the data needed for profiles, vacancies, analytics and department reports. Access depends on the user's role.

sections.title: Data we process
sections.body: The system may store account details, contact information, education data, employment status, workplace, position, resumes, employer records and vacancies.
items: for graduates: full name, group, graduation year, direction, profile, study form, employment status and survey details;
for employers: organization name, contacts, company description and posted vacancies;
for administrators: account data and actions for managing directories, reports and profiles.

sections.title: How data is used
sections.body: Data is used to maintain graduate and employer profiles, publish vacancies, prepare statistics, generate reports and keep the department database up to date.
items: analytics by year, group, direction and employment status;
report export to PDF, Word and Excel;
data review and correction by system administrators.

sections.title: Who can access data
sections.body: Data access is limited by user role. Graduates manage their own profile, employers manage their company and vacancies, and administrators maintain department data and reporting.
items: graduates can view and update their own information;
employers can access data required for vacancies and graduate interaction;
administrators have extended access for database maintenance and reporting.

sections.title: Reports and exports
sections.body: Exported reports may include information about graduates, groups, employment and continuing education. For this reason, report export is limited to administrative users.
items: before sharing a report, the administrator should check its purpose and data scope;
reports should be stored and shared only when there is a work-related need.

sections.title: Technical safeguards
sections.body: The system uses authentication, access tokens and role-based permissions. Account credentials must not be shared with other users.
items: users are responsible for keeping their username and password secure;
suspected unauthorized access should be reported to an administrator;
authorization tokens are stored in the user's browser and removed on logout.

sections.title: Updating and correcting data
sections.body: Users can update profile fields available to their role. If any information is incorrect or requires clarification, contact the department administrator.
items: graduates can update education and employment details in their account;
administrators can correct data required for department reporting.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.subtitle: These terms describe how graduates, employers and administrators should use the system.
terms.summaryText: The system is intended for department employment tracking, employer interaction and reporting. Data should be accurate and used responsibly.

sections.title: Purpose of the system
sections.body: The platform is used to track department graduates, manage employer profiles, publish vacancies and prepare employment analytics reports.
items: graduates fill in their profile and employment information;
employers publish vacancies and update company data;
administrators manage reference data, users and reports.

sections.title: User account
sections.body: Users are responsible for the correctness of registration data and the security of their account.
items: do not share login credentials with other people;
update the profile when education or employment details change;
administrators may clarify or correct data needed for reporting.

sections.title: Content and records
sections.body: Users must not upload false information, unrelated personal data, harmful files, spam or content unrelated to employment and department work.
items: vacancies should clearly describe the position, requirements and conditions;
resumes and documents should relate to the user's profile;
administrators may delete or hide incorrect records.

sections.title: Reports and exports
sections.body: Reports are intended for internal department analytics, certificates, graduate employment monitoring and education program indicators.
items: report filters should match the required year, group, study form or status;
exported files should be used only for their intended purpose;
data should be reviewed before official use of a report.

sections.title: Limitations
sections.body: The system helps centralize data and automate reports, but final analytics quality depends on the completeness and freshness of information entered by users and administrators.
items: employment details need regular updates;
incorrect data should be corrected after it is found;
administrators are responsible for final report checks before use.

sections.title: Changes to these terms
sections.body: These terms may be updated as the system evolves, reporting requirements change or new features are added. The current version is available on this page.
items: changes apply after the updated version is published;
continued use of the system means using it under the current rules.

security.title: Account security
security.subtitle: Change the password to protect access to the dashboard.
security.passwordChangeError: Could not change the password. Check the entered data.
security.recommendationsText: Use a long password with letters, numbers and symbols. Do not share your password with other users.
security.adminNoticeText: The new password replaces the old one immediately. Share it with the user through a secure channel.
